{{Infobox ancient site | name =Nanatsu Kofun cluster | native_name =七ツ森古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = 七ツ森古墳群 - panoramio.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Nanatsu Kofun cluster | map_type = Japan Oita Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Nanatsu Kofun cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|32|57|6.8|N|131|18|36.3|E|region:JP-44|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Taketa, Ōita]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = Kofun custer | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.4th century | abandoned = | epochs =early [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|32|57|6.8|N|131|18|36.3|E|}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Nanatsu Kofun Cluster'''|七ツ森古墳群|}} is a group [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|burial mound]]s, located in the Sugou neighborhood of the city of [[Taketa, Ōita]], on the island of [[Kyushu]] [[Japan]]. The tumuli were collectively designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1959.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/206763|title=七ツ森古墳群|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Nanatsu Kofun cluster is located along [[Japan National Route 57]] on a plateau in western part of Taketa City. It is believed that there were originally seven tumuli as the name suggests, but the only ones that remain today are two round tumuli (Kofun A and D) and two keyhole-shaped tumuli (Kofun B and C).<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Kofun A, located furthest west, is a round {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳||}}-style tumulus with a diameter of 20 meters and a height of four meters.The excavated boat-shaped sarcophagus is 80 cm long and 25 cm wide and has small stone pillows, so it is thought that the tomb was for infant who had just been born.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
Kofun B is a {{nihongo|''[[zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It has a total length of 51 meters, with a posterior circle diameter of 28 meters, height of six meters, and an anterior section with a width of eight meters and a height of three meters. A combination-style [[sarcophagus]] was discovered in the center of the posterior circular portion, containing the remains of two men and a woman. [[Grave goods]] found in Kofun B and C included six [[bronze mirror]]s, ''[[magatama]]'' beads, jasper stone pieces, and dear antler-shaped swords. The jasper pieces were found to be parts of a stone chime, possibly a ''[[bianqing]]'', which had been purposely crushed and its fragments scattered near the remains. Based on the shape of the tumulus, the structure of the sarcophagus, and the excavated items, it is estimated to be the oldest [[Kinai region|Kinai-style]] tumulus in the region, built in the early Kofun period.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site is now a park noted for its large profusion of [[Lycoris radiata|red spider lilies]] in fall. It is located approximately 20 minutes by car from [[Bungo-Taketa Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Hōhi Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ōita)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://taketa.guide/spots/detail/c5a7188b-bfc6-4f41-9aec-05dbbf04cedd Taketa City home page]{{in lang|ja}}

revision.comment: Adding [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:前方後円墳]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site | name =Nanatsu Kofun cluster | native_name =七ツ森古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = 七ツ森古墳群 - panoramio.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Nanatsu Kofun cluster | map_type = Japan Oita Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Nanatsu Kofun cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|32|57|6.8|N|131|18|36.3|E|region:JP-44|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Taketa, Ōita]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = Kofun custer | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.4th century | abandoned = | epochs =early [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|32|57|6.8|N|131|18|36.3|E|}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Nanatsu Kofun Cluster'''|七ツ森古墳群|}} is a group [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|burial mound]]s, located in the Sugou neighborhood of the city of [[Taketa, Ōita]], on the island of [[Kyushu]] [[Japan]]. The tumuli were collectively designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1959.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/206763|title=七ツ森古墳群|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Nanatsu Kofun cluster is located along [[Japan National Route 57]] on a plateau in western part of Taketa City. It is believed that there were originally seven tumuli as the name suggests, but the only ones that remain today are two round tumuli (Kofun A and D) and two keyhole-shaped tumuli (Kofun B and C).<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Kofun A, located furthest west, is a round {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳||}}-style tumulus with a diameter of 20 meters and a height of four meters.The excavated boat-shaped sarcophagus is 80 cm long and 25 cm wide and has small stone pillows, so it is thought that the tomb was for infant who had just been born.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
Kofun B is a {{nihongo|''[[zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It has a total length of 51 meters, with a posterior circle diameter of 28 meters, height of six meters, and an anterior section with a width of eight meters and a height of three meters. A combination-style [[sarcophagus]] was discovered in the center of the posterior circular portion, containing the remains of two men and a woman. [[Grave goods]] found in Kofun B and C included six [[bronze mirror]]s, ''[[magatama]]'' beads, jasper stone pieces, and dear antler-shaped swords. The jasper pieces were found to be parts of a stone chime, possibly a ''[[bianqing]]'', which had been purposely crushed and its fragments scattered near the remains. Based on the shape of the tumulus, the structure of the sarcophagus, and the excavated items, it is estimated to be the oldest [[Kinai region|Kinai-style]] tumulus in the region, built in the early Kofun period.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site is now a park noted for its large profusion of [[Lycoris radiata|red spider lilies]] in fall. It is located approximately 20 minutes by car from [[Bungo-Taketa Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Hōhi Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ōita)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://taketa.guide/spots/detail/c5a7188b-bfc6-4f41-9aec-05dbbf04cedd Taketa City home page]{{in lang|ja}}

The site is now a park noted for its large profusion of [[Lycoris radiata|red spider lilies]] in fall. It is located approximately 20 minutes by car from [[Bungo-Taketa Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Hōhi Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Infobox ancient site | name =Nanatsu Kofun cluster | native_name =七ツ森古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = 七ツ森古墳群 - panoramio.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Nanatsu Kofun cluster | map_type = Japan Oita Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Nanatsu Kofun cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|32|57|6.8|N|131|18|36.3|E|region:JP-44|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Taketa, Ōita]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = Kofun custer | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.4th century | abandoned = | epochs =early [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|32|57|6.8|N|131|18|36.3|E|}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Nanatsu Kofun Cluster'''|七ツ森古墳群|}} is a group [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|burial mound]]s, located in the Sugou neighborhood of the city of [[Taketa, Ōita]], on the island of [[Kyushu]] [[Japan]]. The tumuli were collectively designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1959.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/206763|title=七ツ森古墳群|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Nanatsu Kofun cluster is located along [[Japan National Route 57]] on a plateau in western part of Taketa City. It is believed that there were originally seven tumuli as the name suggests, but the only ones that remain today are two round tumuli (Kofun A and D) and two keyhole-shaped tumuli (Kofun B and C).<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Kofun A, located furthest west, is a round {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳||}}-style tumulus with a diameter of 20 meters and a height of four meters.The excavated boat-shaped sarcophagus is 80 cm long and 25 cm wide and has small stone pillows, so it is thought that the tomb was for infant who had just been born.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
Kofun B is a {{nihongo|''[[zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It has a total length of 51 meters, with a posterior circle diameter of 28 meters, height of six meters, and an anterior section with a width of eight meters and a height of three meters. A combination-style [[sarcophagus]] was discovered in the center of the posterior circular portion, containing the remains of two men and a woman. [[Grave goods]] found in Kofun B and C included six [[bronze mirror]]s, ''[[magatama]]'' beads, jasper stone pieces, and dear antler-shaped swords. The jasper pieces were found to be parts of a stone chime, possibly a ''[[bianqing]]'', which had been purposely crushed and its fragments scattered near the remains. Based on the shape of the tumulus, the structure of the sarcophagus, and the excavated items, it is estimated to be the oldest [[Kinai region|Kinai-style]] tumulus in the region, built in the early Kofun period.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site is now a park noted for its large profusion of [[Lycoris radiata|red spider lilies]] in fall. It is located approximately 20 minutes by car from [[Bungo-Taketa Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Hōhi Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Infobox ancient site | name =Nanatsu Kofun cluster | native_name =七ツ森古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = 七ツ森古墳群 - panoramio.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Nanatsu Kofun cluster | map_type = Japan Oita Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Nanatsu Kofun cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|32|57|6.8|N|131|18|36.3|E|region:JP-44|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Taketa, Ōita]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = Kofun custer | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.4th century | abandoned = | epochs =early [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | other_designation = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|32|57|6.8|N|131|18|36.3|E|}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Nanatsu Kofun Cluster'''|七ツ森古墳群|}} is a group [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|burial mound]]s, located in the Sugou neighborhood of the city of [[Taketa, Ōita]], on the island of [[Kyushu]] [[Japan]]. The tumuli were collectively designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1959.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/206763|title=七ツ森古墳群|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Nanatsu Kofun cluster is located along [[Japan National Route 57]] on a plateau in western part of Taketa City. It is believed that there were originally seven tumuli as the name suggests, but the only ones that remain today are two round tumuli (Kofun A and D) and two keyhole-shaped tumuli (Kofun B and C).<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Kofun A, located furthest west, is a round {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳||}}-style tumulus with a diameter of 20 meters and a height of four meters.The excavated boat-shaped sarcophagus is 80 cm long and 25 cm wide and has small stone pillows, so it is thought that the tomb was for infant who had just been born.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
Kofun B is a {{nihongo|''[[zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It has a total length of 51 meters, with a posterior circle diameter of 28 meters, height of six meters, and an anterior section with a width of eight meters and a height of three meters. A combination-style [[sarcophagus]] was discovered in the center of the posterior circular portion, containing the remains of two men and a woman. [[Grave goods]] found in Kofun B and C included six [[bronze mirror]]s, ''[[magatama]]'' beads, jasper stone pieces, and dear antler-shaped swords. The jasper pieces were found to be parts of a stone chime, possibly a ''[[bianqing]]'', which had been purposely crushed and its fragments scattered near the remains. Based on the shape of the tumulus, the structure of the sarcophagus, and the excavated items, it is estimated to be the oldest [[Kinai region|Kinai-style]] tumulus in the region, built in the early Kofun period.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site is now a park noted for its large profusion of [[Lycoris radiata|red spider lilies]] in fall. It is located approximately 20 minutes by car from [[Bungo-Taketa Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Hōhi Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ōita)]]

It is believed that there were originally seven tumuli as the name suggests, but the only ones that remain today are two round tumuli (Kofun A and D) and two keyhole-shaped tumuli (Kofun B and C).<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Kofun A, located furthest west, is a round {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳||}}-style tumulus with a diameter of 20 meters and a height of four meters.The excavated boat-shaped sarcophagus is 80 cm long and 25 cm wide and has small stone pillows, so it is thought that the tomb was for infant who had just been born.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
Kofun B is a {{nihongo|''[[zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It has a total length of 51 meters, with a posterior circle diameter of 28 meters, height of six meters, and an anterior section with a width of eight meters and a height of three meters. A combination-style [[sarcophagus]] was discovered in the center of the posterior circular portion, containing the remains of two men and a woman. [[Grave goods]] found in Kofun B and C included six [[bronze mirror]]s, ''[[magatama]]'' beads, jasper stone pieces, and dear antler-shaped swords. The jasper pieces were found to be parts of a stone chime, possibly a ''[[bianqing]]'', which had been purposely crushed and its fragments scattered near the remains. Based on the shape of the tumulus, the structure of the sarcophagus, and the excavated items, it is estimated to be the oldest [[Kinai region|Kinai-style]] tumulus in the region, built in the early Kofun period.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site is now a park noted for its large profusion of [[Lycoris radiata|red spider lilies]] in fall. It is located approximately 20 minutes by car from [[Bungo-Taketa Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Hōhi Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{Infobox ancient site | name =Nanatsu Kofun cluster | native_name =七ツ森古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = 七ツ森古墳群 - panoramio.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Nanatsu Kofun cluster | map_type = Japan Oita Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Nanatsu Kofun cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{coord|32|57|6.8|N|131|18|36.3|E|region:JP-44|display=inline, title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[Taketa, Ōita]], [[Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = Kofun custer | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.4th century | abandoned = | epochs =early [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site = | website = <!-- {{URL|example.com}} --> | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|32|57|6.8|N|131|18|36.3|E|}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Nanatsu Kofun Cluster'''|七ツ森古墳群|}} is a group [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]]s, located in the Sugou neighborhood of the city of [[Taketa, Ōita]], on the island of [[Kyushu]] [[Japan]]. The tumuli were collectively designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1959.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/206763|title=七ツ森古墳群|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Nanatsu Kofun cluster is located along [[Japan National Route 57]] on a plateau in western part of Taketa City. It is believed that there were originally seven tumuli as the name suggests, but the only ones that remain today are two round tumuli (Kofun A and D) and two keyhole-shaped tumuli (Kofun B and C).<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Kofun A, located furthest west, is a round {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun||}}-style tumulus with a diameter of 20 meters and a height of four meters.The excavated boat-shaped sarcophagus is 80 cm long and 25 cm wide and has small stone pillows, so it is thought that the tomb was for infant who had just been born.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
Kofun B is a {{nihongo|''[[zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It has a total length of 51 meters, with a posterior circle diameter of 28 meters, height of six meters, and an anterior section with a width of eight meters and a height of three meters. A combination-style [[sarcophagus]] was discovered in the center of the posterior circular portion, containing the remains of two men and a woman. [[Grave goods]] found in Kofun B and C included six [[bronze mirror]]s, ''[[magatama]]'' beads, jasper stone pieces, and dear antler-shaped swords. The jasper pieces were found to be parts of a stone chime, possibly a ''[[bianqing]]'', which had been purposely crushed and its fragments scattered near the remains. Based on the shape of the tumulus, the structure of the sarcophagus, and the excavated items, it is estimated to be the oldest [[Kinai region|Kinai-style]] tumulus in the region, built in the early Kofun period.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site is now a park noted for its large profusion of [[Lycoris radiata|red spider lilies]] in fall. It is located approximately 20 minutes by car from [[Bungo-Taketa Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Hōhi Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ōita)]]

{{nihongo|'''Nanatsu Kofun Cluster'''|七ツ森古墳群|}} is a group [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]]s, located in the Sugou neighborhood of the city of [[Taketa, Ōita]], on the island of [[Kyushu]] [[Japan]]. The tumuli were collectively designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1959.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/206763|title=七ツ森古墳群|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Nanatsu Kofun cluster is located along [[Japan National Route 57]] on a plateau in western part of Taketa City. It is believed that there were originally seven tumuli as the name suggests, but the only ones that remain today are two round tumuli (Kofun A and D) and two keyhole-shaped tumuli (Kofun B and C).<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Kofun A, located furthest west, is a round {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun||}}-style tumulus with a diameter of 20 meters and a height of four meters.The excavated boat-shaped sarcophagus is 80 cm long and 25 cm wide and has small stone pillows, so it is thought that the tomb was for infant who had just been born.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
Kofun B is a {{nihongo|''[[zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It has a total length of 51 meters, with a posterior circle diameter of 28 meters, height of six meters, and an anterior section with a width of eight meters and a height of three meters. A combination-style [[sarcophagus]] was discovered in the center of the posterior circular portion, containing the remains of two men and a woman. [[Grave goods]] found in Kofun B and C included six [[bronze mirror]]s, ''[[magatama]]'' beads, jasper stone pieces, and dear antler-shaped swords. The jasper pieces were found to be parts of a stone chime, possibly a ''[[bianqing]]'', which had been purposely crushed and its fragments scattered near the remains. Based on the shape of the tumulus, the structure of the sarcophagus, and the excavated items, it is estimated to be the oldest [[Kinai region|Kinai-style]] tumulus in the region, built in the early Kofun period.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site is now a park noted for its large profusion of [[Lycoris radiata|red spider lilies]] in fall. It is located approximately 20 minutes by car from [[Bungo-Taketa Station]] on the [[JR Kyushu]] [[Hōhi Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ōita)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://taketa.guide/spots/detail/c5a7188b-bfc6-4f41-9aec-05dbbf04cedd Taketa City home page]{{in lang|ja}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nanatsumori Kofun Cluster]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nanatsumori Kofun Cluster]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:kk]] [[Category:History of Ōita Prefecture]] [[Category:Taketa, Ōita]] [[Category:Historic Sites of Japan]] [[Category:Kofun clusters]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:円墳]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:completion tier 4]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] }} [[en:Nanatsumori Kofun Cluster]] [[sn:Nanatsumori Kofun Cluster]] {{citation needed|Nanatsumori Kofun Cluster}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]]

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Nanatsumori Kofun Cluster]][[ja:七ツ森古墳群]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11353353]] Wikidata name: Nanatsumori Kofun Cluster [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:27, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name =Nanatsu Kofun cluster | native_name =七ツ森古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = 七ツ森古墳群 - panoramio.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Nanatsu Kofun cluster | map_type = Japan Oita Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Nanatsu Kofun cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates ={{coord|32|57|6.8|N|131|18|36.3|E|region:JP-44|display=inline, title}}; {{Coord|32.95188888888889|N|131.31008333333332|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Taketa, Ōita|Taketa, Ōita]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = Kofun custer | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.4th century | abandoned = | epochs =early [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site =; {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|WD=Q30834580}}| website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|32|57|6.8|N|131|18|36.3|E|}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Nanatsu Kofun Cluster'''|七ツ森古墳群|}} is a group [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]]s, located in the Sugou neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Taketa, Ōita|Taketa, Ōita]], on the island of [[Kyushu]] [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumuli were collectively designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1959.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/206763|title=七ツ森古墳群|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Nanatsu Kofun cluster is located along [[:en:Japan National Route 57|Japan National Route 57]] on a plateau in western part of Taketa City. It is believed that there were originally seven tumuli as the name suggests, but the only ones that remain today are two round tumuli (Kofun A and D) and two keyhole-shaped tumuli (Kofun B and C).<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Kofun A, located furthest west, is a round {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun||}}-style tumulus with a diameter of 20 meters and a height of four meters.The excavated boat-shaped sarcophagus is 80 cm long and 25 cm wide and has small stone pillows, so it is thought that the tomb was for infant who had just been born.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
Kofun B is a {{nihongo|''[[zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It has a total length of 51 meters, with a posterior circle diameter of 28 meters, height of six meters, and an anterior section with a width of eight meters and a height of three meters. A combination-style [[:en:Sarcophagus|Sarcophagus]] was discovered in the center of the posterior circular portion, containing the remains of two men and a woman. [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] found in Kofun B and C included six [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]]s, ''[[magatama]]'' beads, jasper stone pieces, and dear antler-shaped swords. The jasper pieces were found to be parts of a stone chime, possibly a ''[[:en:bianqing|bianqing]]'', which had been purposely crushed and its fragments scattered near the remains. Based on the shape of the tumulus, the structure of the sarcophagus, and the excavated items, it is estimated to be the oldest [[:en:Kinai region|Kinai-style]] tumulus in the region, built in the early Kofun period.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site is now a park noted for its large profusion of [[:en:Lycoris radiata|red spider lilies]] in fall. It is located approximately 20 minutes by car from [[:en:Bungo-Taketa Station|Bungo-Taketa Station]] on the [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|Kyushu Railway Company]] [[:en:Hōhi Main Line|Hōhi Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ōita)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ōita)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://taketa.guide/spots/detail/c5a7188b-bfc6-4f41-9aec-05dbbf04cedd Taketa City home page]{{in lang|ja}}

[[Category:Completion tier 4]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Nanatsumori Kofun Cluster]][[ja:七ツ森古墳群]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11353353]] Wikidata name: Nanatsumori Kofun Cluster [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:27, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name =Nanatsu Kofun cluster | native_name =七ツ森古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = 七ツ森古墳群 - panoramio.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Nanatsu Kofun cluster | map_type = Japan Oita Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Nanatsu Kofun cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates ={{coord|32|57|6.8|N|131|18|36.3|E|region:JP-44|display=inline, title}}; {{Coord|32.95188888888889|N|131.31008333333332|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Taketa, Ōita|Taketa, Ōita]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = Kofun custer | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.4th century | abandoned = | epochs =early [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site =; {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|WD=Q30834580}}| website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|32|57|6.8|N|131|18|36.3|E|}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Nanatsu Kofun Cluster'''|七ツ森古墳群|}} is a group [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]]s, located in the Sugou neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Taketa, Ōita|Taketa, Ōita]], on the island of [[Kyushu]] [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumuli were collectively designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1959.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/206763|title=七ツ森古墳群|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Nanatsu Kofun cluster is located along [[:en:Japan National Route 57|Japan National Route 57]] on a plateau in western part of Taketa City. It is believed that there were originally seven tumuli as the name suggests, but the only ones that remain today are two round tumuli (Kofun A and D) and two keyhole-shaped tumuli (Kofun B and C).<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Kofun A, located furthest west, is a round {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun||}}-style tumulus with a diameter of 20 meters and a height of four meters.The excavated boat-shaped sarcophagus is 80 cm long and 25 cm wide and has small stone pillows, so it is thought that the tomb was for infant who had just been born.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
Kofun B is a {{nihongo|''[[zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It has a total length of 51 meters, with a posterior circle diameter of 28 meters, height of six meters, and an anterior section with a width of eight meters and a height of three meters. A combination-style [[:en:Sarcophagus|Sarcophagus]] was discovered in the center of the posterior circular portion, containing the remains of two men and a woman. [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] found in Kofun B and C included six [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]]s, ''[[magatama]]'' beads, jasper stone pieces, and dear antler-shaped swords. The jasper pieces were found to be parts of a stone chime, possibly a ''[[:en:bianqing|bianqing]]'', which had been purposely crushed and its fragments scattered near the remains. Based on the shape of the tumulus, the structure of the sarcophagus, and the excavated items, it is estimated to be the oldest [[:en:Kinai region|Kinai-style]] tumulus in the region, built in the early Kofun period.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site is now a park noted for its large profusion of [[:en:Lycoris radiata|red spider lilies]] in fall. It is located approximately 20 minutes by car from [[:en:Bungo-Taketa Station|Bungo-Taketa Station]] on the [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|Kyushu Railway Company]] [[:en:Hōhi Main Line|Hōhi Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ōita)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ōita)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://taketa.guide/spots/detail/c5a7188b-bfc6-4f41-9aec-05dbbf04cedd Taketa City home page]{{in lang|ja}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Nanatsumori Kofun Cluster]][[ja:七ツ森古墳群]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11353353]] Wikidata name: Nanatsumori Kofun Cluster [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:27, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name =Nanatsu Kofun cluster | native_name =七ツ森古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = 七ツ森古墳群 - panoramio.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Nanatsu Kofun cluster | map_type = Japan Oita Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Nanatsu Kofun cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates ={{coord|32|57|6.8|N|131|18|36.3|E|region:JP-44|display=inline, title}}; {{Coord|32.95188888888889|N|131.31008333333332|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Taketa, Ōita|Taketa, Ōita]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = Kofun custer | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.4th century | abandoned = | epochs =early [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site =; {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|WD=Q30834580}}| website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|32|57|6.8|N|131|18|36.3|E|}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Nanatsu Kofun Cluster'''|七ツ森古墳群|}} is a group [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]]s, located in the Sugou neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Taketa, Ōita|Taketa, Ōita]], on the island of [[Kyushu]] [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumuli were collectively designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1959.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/206763|title=七ツ森古墳群|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Nanatsu Kofun cluster is located along [[:en:Japan National Route 57|Japan National Route 57]] on a plateau in western part of Taketa City. It is believed that there were originally seven tumuli as the name suggests, but the only ones that remain today are two round tumuli (Kofun A and D) and two keyhole-shaped tumuli (Kofun B and C).<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Kofun A, located furthest west, is a round {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun||}}-style tumulus with a diameter of 20 meters and a height of four meters.The excavated boat-shaped sarcophagus is 80 cm long and 25 cm wide and has small stone pillows, so it is thought that the tomb was for infant who had just been born.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
Kofun B is a {{nihongo|''[[zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It has a total length of 51 meters, with a posterior circle diameter of 28 meters, height of six meters, and an anterior section with a width of eight meters and a height of three meters. A combination-style [[:en:Sarcophagus|Sarcophagus]] was discovered in the center of the posterior circular portion, containing the remains of two men and a woman. [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] found in Kofun B and C included six [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]]s, ''[[magatama]]'' beads, jasper stone pieces, and dear antler-shaped swords. The jasper pieces were found to be parts of a stone chime, possibly a ''[[:en:bianqing|bianqing]]'', which had been purposely crushed and its fragments scattered near the remains. Based on the shape of the tumulus, the structure of the sarcophagus, and the excavated items, it is estimated to be the oldest [[:en:Kinai region|Kinai-style]] tumulus in the region, built in the early Kofun period.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site is now a park noted for its large profusion of [[:en:Lycoris radiata|red spider lilies]] in fall. It is located approximately 20 minutes by car from [[:en:Bungo-Taketa Station|Bungo-Taketa Station]] on the [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|Kyushu Railway Company]] [[:en:Hōhi Main Line|Hōhi Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ōita)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ōita)]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Nanatsumori Kofun Cluster]][[ja:七ツ森古墳群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Nanatsumori Kofun Cluster]][[ja:七ツ森古墳群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Nanatsumori Kofun Cluster]][[ja:七ツ森古墳群]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11353353]] Wikidata name: Nanatsumori Kofun Cluster [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:27, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name =Nanatsu Kofun cluster | native_name =七ツ森古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = 七ツ森古墳群 - panoramio.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Nanatsu Kofun cluster | map_type = Japan Oita Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Nanatsu Kofun cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates ={{coord|32|57|6.8|N|131|18|36.3|E|region:JP-44|display=inline, title}}; {{Coord|32.95188888888889|N|131.31008333333332|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Taketa, Ōita|Taketa, Ōita]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]] | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = Kofun custer | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.4th century | abandoned = | epochs =early [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site =; {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|WD=Q30834580}}| website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|32|57|6.8|N|131|18|36.3|E|}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Nanatsu Kofun Cluster'''|七ツ森古墳群|}} is a group [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]]s, located in the Sugou neighborhood of the city of [[:en:Taketa, Ōita|Taketa, Ōita]], on the island of [[Kyushu]] [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumuli were collectively designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1959.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/206763|title=七ツ森古墳群|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Nanatsu Kofun cluster is located along [[:en:Japan National Route 57|Japan National Route 57]] on a plateau in western part of Taketa City. It is believed that there were originally seven tumuli as the name suggests, but the only ones that remain today are two round tumuli (Kofun A and D) and two keyhole-shaped tumuli (Kofun B and C).<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Kofun A, located furthest west, is a round {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun||}}-style tumulus with a diameter of 20 meters and a height of four meters.The excavated boat-shaped sarcophagus is 80 cm long and 25 cm wide and has small stone pillows, so it is thought that the tomb was for infant who had just been born.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
Kofun B is a {{nihongo|''[[zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It has a total length of 51 meters, with a posterior circle diameter of 28 meters, height of six meters, and an anterior section with a width of eight meters and a height of three meters. A combination-style [[:en:Sarcophagus|Sarcophagus]] was discovered in the center of the posterior circular portion, containing the remains of two men and a woman. [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] found in Kofun B and C included six [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]]s, ''[[magatama]]'' beads, jasper stone pieces, and dear antler-shaped swords. The jasper pieces were found to be parts of a stone chime, possibly a ''[[:en:bianqing|bianqing]]'', which had been purposely crushed and its fragments scattered near the remains. Based on the shape of the tumulus, the structure of the sarcophagus, and the excavated items, it is estimated to be the oldest [[:en:Kinai region|Kinai-style]] tumulus in the region, built in the early Kofun period.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site is now a park noted for its large profusion of [[:en:Lycoris radiata|red spider lilies]] in fall. It is located approximately 20 minutes by car from [[:en:Bungo-Taketa Station|Bungo-Taketa Station]] on the [[:en:Kyushu Railway Company|Kyushu Railway Company]] [[:en:Hōhi Main Line|Hōhi Main Line]].<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *[[:en:List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ōita)|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ōita)]]

revision.comment: v25: Convert interwiki links [[:en:...]] to {{ill}} templates
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Nanatsumori Kofun Cluster]][[ja:七ツ森古墳群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Nanatsumori Kofun Cluster]][[ja:七ツ森古墳群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Nanatsumori Kofun Cluster]][[ja:七ツ森古墳群]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11353353]] Wikidata name: Nanatsumori Kofun Cluster [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:27, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site | name =Nanatsu Kofun cluster | native_name =七ツ森古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = 七ツ森古墳群 - panoramio.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Nanatsu Kofun cluster | map_type = Japan Oita Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Nanatsu Kofun cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates ={{coord|32|57|6.8|N|131|18|36.3|E|region:JP-44|display=inline, title}}; {{Coord|32.95188888888889|N|131.31008333333332|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Taketa, Ōita|en|Taketa, Ōita|WD=Q850508|lt=Taketa, Ōita}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}} | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = Kofun custer | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.4th century | abandoned = | epochs =early [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site =; {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|WD=Q30834580}}| website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|32|57|6.8|N|131|18|36.3|E|}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Nanatsu Kofun Cluster'''|七ツ森古墳群|}} is a group [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]]s, located in the Sugou neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Taketa, Ōita|en|Taketa, Ōita|WD=Q850508|lt=Taketa, Ōita}}, on the island of [[Kyushu]] {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The tumuli were collectively designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1959.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/206763|title=七ツ森古墳群|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Nanatsu Kofun cluster is located along {{ill|Japan National Route 57|en|Japan National Route 57|WD=Q6157740|lt=Japan National Route 57}} on a plateau in western part of Taketa City. It is believed that there were originally seven tumuli as the name suggests, but the only ones that remain today are two round tumuli (Kofun A and D) and two keyhole-shaped tumuli (Kofun B and C).<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Kofun A, located furthest west, is a round {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun||}}-style tumulus with a diameter of 20 meters and a height of four meters.The excavated boat-shaped sarcophagus is 80 cm long and 25 cm wide and has small stone pillows, so it is thought that the tomb was for infant who had just been born.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
Kofun B is a {{nihongo|''[[zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It has a total length of 51 meters, with a posterior circle diameter of 28 meters, height of six meters, and an anterior section with a width of eight meters and a height of three meters. A combination-style {{ill|Sarcophagus|en|Sarcophagus|WD=Q48634|lt=Sarcophagus}} was discovered in the center of the posterior circular portion, containing the remains of two men and a woman. {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods}} found in Kofun B and C included six {{ill|Bronze mirror|en|Bronze mirror|WD=Q1041984|lt=Bronze mirror}}s, ''[[magatama]]'' beads, jasper stone pieces, and dear antler-shaped swords. The jasper pieces were found to be parts of a stone chime, possibly a ''{{ill|bianqing|en|bianqing|WD=Q1744337|lt=bianqing}}'', which had been purposely crushed and its fragments scattered near the remains. Based on the shape of the tumulus, the structure of the sarcophagus, and the excavated items, it is estimated to be the oldest [[:en:Kinai region|Kinai-style]] tumulus in the region, built in the early Kofun period.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site is now a park noted for its large profusion of {{ill|Lycoris radiata|en|Lycoris radiata|WD=Q161828|lt=red spider lilies}} in fall. It is located approximately 20 minutes by car from {{ill|Bungo-Taketa Station|en|Bungo-Taketa Station|WD=Q4997670|lt=Bungo-Taketa Station}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Railway Company|en|Kyushu Railway Company|WD=Q498366|lt=Kyushu Railway Company}} {{ill|Hōhi Main Line|en|Hōhi Main Line|WD=Q870826|lt=Hōhi Main Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ōita)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ōita)|WD=Q6573294|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ōita)}}
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== *[https://taketa.guide/spots/detail/c5a7188b-bfc6-4f41-9aec-05dbbf04cedd Taketa City home page]{{in lang|ja}}
{{Kofun navbar}}

Kofun B is a {{nihongo|''[[zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. A combination-style {{ill|Sarcophagus|en|Sarcophagus|WD=Q48634|lt=Sarcophagus}} was discovered in the center of the posterior circular portion, containing the remains of two men and a woman. {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods}} found in Kofun B and C included six {{ill|Bronze mirror|en|Bronze mirror|WD=Q1041984|lt=Bronze mirror}}s, ''[[magatama]]'' beads, jasper stone pieces, and dear antler-shaped swords. The jasper pieces were found to be parts of a stone chime, possibly a ''{{ill|bianqing|en|bianqing|WD=Q1744337|lt=bianqing}}'', which had been purposely crushed and its fragments scattered near the remains. Based on the shape of the tumulus, the structure of the sarcophagus, and the excavated items, it is estimated to be the oldest [[:en:Kinai region|Kinai-style]] tumulus in the region, built in the early Kofun period.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site is now a park noted for its large profusion of {{ill|Lycoris radiata|en|Lycoris radiata|WD=Q161828|lt=red spider lilies}} in fall. It is located approximately 20 minutes by car from {{ill|Bungo-Taketa Station|en|Bungo-Taketa Station|WD=Q4997670|lt=Bungo-Taketa Station}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Railway Company|en|Kyushu Railway Company|WD=Q498366|lt=Kyushu Railway Company}} {{ill|Hōhi Main Line|en|Hōhi Main Line|WD=Q870826|lt=Hōhi Main Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

The tumuli were collectively designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1959.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/206763|title=七ツ森古墳群|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Nanatsu Kofun cluster is located along {{ill|Japan National Route 57|en|Japan National Route 57|WD=Q6157740|lt=Japan National Route 57}} on a plateau in western part of Taketa City. The jasper pieces were found to be parts of a stone chime, possibly a ''{{ill|bianqing|en|bianqing|WD=Q1744337|lt=bianqing}}'', which had been purposely crushed and its fragments scattered near the remains. Based on the shape of the tumulus, the structure of the sarcophagus, and the excavated items, it is estimated to be the oldest [[:en:Kinai region|Kinai-style]] tumulus in the region, built in the early Kofun period.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

It is located approximately 20 minutes by car from {{ill|Bungo-Taketa Station|en|Bungo-Taketa Station|WD=Q4997670|lt=Bungo-Taketa Station}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Railway Company|en|Kyushu Railway Company|WD=Q498366|lt=Kyushu Railway Company}} {{ill|Hōhi Main Line|en|Hōhi Main Line|WD=Q870826|lt=Hōhi Main Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>

{{nihongo|'''Nanatsu Kofun Cluster'''|七ツ森古墳群|}} is a group [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]]s, located in the Sugou neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Taketa, Ōita|en|Taketa, Ōita|WD=Q850508|lt=Taketa, Ōita|1=Taketa, Ōita}}, on the island of [[Kyushu]] {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumuli were collectively designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1959.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/206763|title=七ツ森古墳群|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Nanatsu Kofun cluster is located along {{ill|Japan National Route 57|en|Japan National Route 57|WD=Q6157740|lt=Japan National Route 57|1=Japan National Route 57}} on a plateau in western part of Taketa City. It is believed that there were originally seven tumuli as the name suggests, but the only ones that remain today are two round tumuli (Kofun A and D) and two keyhole-shaped tumuli (Kofun B and C).<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Kofun A, located furthest west, is a round {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun||}}-style tumulus with a diameter of 20 meters and a height of four meters.The excavated boat-shaped sarcophagus is 80 cm long and 25 cm wide and has small stone pillows, so it is thought that the tomb was for infant who had just been born.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
Kofun B is a {{nihongo|''[[zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It has a total length of 51 meters, with a posterior circle diameter of 28 meters, height of six meters, and an anterior section with a width of eight meters and a height of three meters. A combination-style {{ill|Sarcophagus|en|Sarcophagus|WD=Q48634|lt=Sarcophagus|1=Sarcophagus}} was discovered in the center of the posterior circular portion, containing the remains of two men and a woman. {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}} found in Kofun B and C included six {{ill|Bronze mirror|en|Bronze mirror|WD=Q1041984|lt=Bronze mirror|1=Bronze mirror}}s, ''[[magatama]]'' beads, jasper stone pieces, and dear antler-shaped swords. The jasper pieces were found to be parts of a stone chime, possibly a ''{{ill|bianqing|en|bianqing|WD=Q1744337|lt=bianqing|1=Bianqing}}'', which had been purposely crushed and its fragments scattered near the remains. Based on the shape of the tumulus, the structure of the sarcophagus, and the excavated items, it is estimated to be the oldest [[:en:Kinai region|Kinai-style]] tumulus in the region, built in the early Kofun period.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site is now a park noted for its large profusion of {{ill|Lycoris radiata|en|Lycoris radiata|WD=Q161828|lt=red spider lilies|1=Lycoris radiata}} in fall. It is located approximately 20 minutes by car from {{ill|Bungo-Taketa Station|en|Bungo-Taketa Station|WD=Q4997670|lt=Bungo-Taketa Station|1=Bungo-Taketa Station}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Railway Company|en|Kyushu Railway Company|WD=Q498366|lt=Kyushu Railway Company|1=Kyushu Railway Company}} {{ill|Hōhi Main Line|en|Hōhi Main Line|WD=Q870826|lt=Hōhi Main Line|1=Hōhi Main Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ōita)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ōita)|WD=Q6573294|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ōita)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ōita)}}

A combination-style {{ill|Sarcophagus|en|Sarcophagus|WD=Q48634|lt=Sarcophagus|1=Sarcophagus}} was discovered in the center of the posterior circular portion, containing the remains of two men and a woman. {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}} found in Kofun B and C included six {{ill|Bronze mirror|en|Bronze mirror|WD=Q1041984|lt=Bronze mirror|1=Bronze mirror}}s, ''[[magatama]]'' beads, jasper stone pieces, and dear antler-shaped swords. The jasper pieces were found to be parts of a stone chime, possibly a ''{{ill|bianqing|en|bianqing|WD=Q1744337|lt=bianqing|1=Bianqing}}'', which had been purposely crushed and its fragments scattered near the remains. Based on the shape of the tumulus, the structure of the sarcophagus, and the excavated items, it is estimated to be the oldest [[:en:Kinai region|Kinai-style]] tumulus in the region, built in the early Kofun period.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site is now a park noted for its large profusion of {{ill|Lycoris radiata|en|Lycoris radiata|WD=Q161828|lt=red spider lilies|1=Lycoris radiata}} in fall. It is located approximately 20 minutes by car from {{ill|Bungo-Taketa Station|en|Bungo-Taketa Station|WD=Q4997670|lt=Bungo-Taketa Station|1=Bungo-Taketa Station}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Railway Company|en|Kyushu Railway Company|WD=Q498366|lt=Kyushu Railway Company|1=Kyushu Railway Company}} {{ill|Hōhi Main Line|en|Hōhi Main Line|WD=Q870826|lt=Hōhi Main Line|1=Hōhi Main Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ōita)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ōita)|WD=Q6573294|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ōita)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Ōita)}}

{{Infobox ancient site | name =Nanatsu Kofun cluster | native_name =七ツ森古墳群 | native_name_lang = | image = 七ツ森古墳群 - panoramio.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Nanatsu Kofun cluster | map_type = Japan Oita Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Nanatsu Kofun cluster | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates ={{coord|32|57|6.8|N|131|18|36.3|E|region:JP-44|display=inline, title}}; {{Coord|32.95188888888889|N|131.31008333333332|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}| map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Taketa, Ōita|en|Taketa, Ōita|WD=Q850508|lt=Taketa, Ōita|1=Taketa, Ōita}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} | region = [[Kyushu]] | type = Kofun custer | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.4th century | abandoned = | epochs =early [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no facilities) | Historic Site =; {{ill|Historic Site of Japan|WD=Q30834580|1=Historic Site of Japan}}| website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|32|57|6.8|N|131|18|36.3|E|}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Nanatsu Kofun Cluster'''|七ツ森古墳群|}} is a group [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun|Kofun]]s, located in the Sugou neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Taketa, Ōita|en|Taketa, Ōita|WD=Q850508|lt=Taketa, Ōita|1=Taketa, Ōita}}, on the island of [[Kyushu]] {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumuli were collectively designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1959.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/206763|title=七ツ森古墳群|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2023}}</ref>
==Overview== The Nanatsu Kofun cluster is located along {{ill|Japan National Route 57|en|Japan National Route 57|WD=Q6157740|lt=Japan National Route 57|1=Japan National Route 57}} on a plateau in western part of Taketa City. It is believed that there were originally seven tumuli as the name suggests, but the only ones that remain today are two round tumuli (Kofun A and D) and two keyhole-shaped tumuli (Kofun B and C).<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=4311750404}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
Kofun A, located furthest west, is a round {{nihongo|''enpun''|円墳|1=Round Kofun||}}-style tumulus with a diameter of 20 meters and a height of four meters.The excavated boat-shaped sarcophagus is 80 cm long and 25 cm wide and has small stone pillows, so it is thought that the tomb was for infant who had just been born.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
Kofun B is a {{nihongo|''[[zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpōkōenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpōkōenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpōkōenfun|1=[[Zenpōkōenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above. It has a total length of 51 meters, with a posterior circle diameter of 28 meters, height of six meters, and an anterior section with a width of eight meters and a height of three meters. A combination-style {{ill|Sarcophagus|en|Sarcophagus|WD=Q48634|lt=Sarcophagus|1=Sarcophagus}} was discovered in the center of the posterior circular portion, containing the remains of two men and a woman. {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}} found in Kofun B and C included six {{ill|Bronze mirror|en|Bronze mirror|WD=Q1041984|lt=Bronze mirror|1=Bronze mirror}}s, ''[[magatama]]'' beads, jasper stone pieces, and dear antler-shaped swords. The jasper pieces were found to be parts of a stone chime, possibly a ''{{ill|bianqing|en|bianqing|WD=Q1744337|lt=bianqing|1=Bianqing}}'', which had been purposely crushed and its fragments scattered near the remains. Based on the shape of the tumulus, the structure of the sarcophagus, and the excavated items, it is estimated to be the oldest [[:en:Kinai region|Kinai-style]] tumulus in the region, built in the early Kofun period.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The site is now a park noted for its large profusion of {{ill|Lycoris radiata|en|Lycoris radiata|WD=Q161828|lt=red spider lilies|1=Lycoris radiata}} in fall. It is located approximately 20 minutes by car from {{ill|Bungo-Taketa Station|en|Bungo-Taketa Station|WD=Q4997670|lt=Bungo-Taketa Station|1=Bungo-Taketa Station}} on the {{ill|Kyushu Railway Company|en|Kyushu Railway Company|WD=Q498366|lt=Kyushu Railway Company|1=Kyushu Railway Company}} {{ill|Hōhi Main Line|en|Hōhi Main Line|WD=Q870826|lt=Hōhi Main Line|1=Hōhi Main Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
